info.name: Landgrab
info.attribution:
Layout, board, cards, design, and coding by Jonathan Cox (email: Contact@GettingGames.com for inquiries) and released to the Public Domain under CC0.

The library image, expand icon, and wall icon by Jonathan Cox are released to the Public Domain under CC0.

Attribution for other icons are contained in the About overlay.

Sound while changing turns is "Wrong Answer" by UNIVERSFIELD and is available under the Pixabay License at  https://pixabay.com/sound-effects/wrong-answer-129254/.
info.description: Grab the most valuable land by building walls, and take from your opponents when you are stronger.
info.similarAwards:
2003 Spiel der Spiele Hit mit Freunden Recommended
2003 Meeples Choice Award Nominee
2003 Japan Boardgame Prize Best Advanced Game Nominee
2003 International Gamers Awards - General Strategy; Multi-player Nominee
info.helpText:
Players take seats. Press the Setup button to begin. 

Detailed usability information is found in the bottom middle of the page by hovering over the information boxes.

arguments.addendum: ${player_name} must draw a card from the deck or market before ending their turn.
${player_name} performs card action(s) from the left or right side and spends $${cost} [ ${previous_money} -> ${total_money} ]

victory_threshold_label1.text: Otherwise, Score for money majority at the end of the game:

endgame.detail:
Game End
The game ends in one of two ways:

Immediate: If any player reaches 30/40 points (4p/3p) then they immediately win the game.

End of Deck: Once the draw deck is empty, remove all cards from the market. Any sold cards are removed from the game, you no longer draw at the end of your turn. Continue playing until all held cards are played. Then the player(s) with the most money gains 5 points, second-most money player(s) get 3 points. Most money wins. If there is a tie, most money wins.

gameplay.detail:
Setup
After clicking 'Setup', each player (in turn order) places one castle and one knight next to it, onto the board. Same-color castles must always be at least 6 orthogonal spaces from each other. If a knight is placed on trees, you must pay $1.

The final player to place a castle immediately takes the first turn of the game.

Gaining & Spending Money: Click on your money total and enter a positive or negative number to change your money total by that amount.
Gaining & Spending Points: Click on your points total and enter a positive or negative number to change your points total by that amount.

2 Player Setup: During each setup turn also place a neutral castle and knight next to it.
Gameplay
On your turn, take one of your cards and drag it into the red or green box in the bottom right corner of the room. Finish your turn by drawing a card from the Draw Deck or from the face-up Market.

Perform Actions (red): Money equal to the red number on the card is automatically reduced from your supply. Perform all actions within the bottom of the card. If there is a vertical line, only choose actions on one of the sides.

Sell Card (green): Money equal to the green number on the card is automatically added to your supply. You cannot draw this card at the end of your turn. This card will be automatically moved to the market once the turn button is pressed.

actions.detail:
Actions
Place Wall: Take a wall from the supply in the middle right side of the screen and place it on the board. The wall can be placed anywhere, except between two same-colored pieces or inside of a domain.

Expand: Move and place walls so that one of your domains extends by one space. If this extends into an opposing domain, your domain must have more knights in it than the opposing domain. After expanding, add/remove points and/or mines for each player.

Place a Knight: Place a knight next to one of your knights or castles. Can only be placed in empty or tree spaces (spend $1 if placed on trees).

Alliance: Between two domains, neither can expand into each other for the rest of the game. Show this by left clicking on any wall between the two domains to rotate it 90 degrees.

Desserter: Between two domains, remove one opposing knight and place one of yours into your domain of the two. Cannot remove a knight that would break a chain of opposing knights back to their castle.

domains.detail:
Creating Domains
As soon as there is a single castle within a walled off area, it becomes a domain and immediately scores points for each of the following within it:

Trees: 1 point
Towns: 3 points
Capital: 5 point

For each mine inside a domain, click the plus above that type of mine in your area to have income and mine majority points automaticaly calculated. You will automatically gain your indicated mine income at the start of each of your turns.
Mine Majority (3 or 4 of the same): 5 points
